revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 7 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月7日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 7일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 7 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初七]][[zh:三月初七]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839096]] Wikidata name: 7th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 7|qq=March 7}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|2|3|4|2|3|4|3|0303|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 7 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月7日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 7일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 7 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初七]][[zh:三月初七]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839096]] Wikidata name: 7th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 7|qq=March 7}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1583|1583]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]) caused the fall of [[:en:Shibata Katsuie|Kashida Katsuie]]’s forces by capturing the castle at [[:en:Ise|Ise Province]]’s [[:en:Kameyama Castle (Mie)|Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)]] in a prelude to the [[:en:Battle of Shizugatake|Battle of Shizugatake]]. * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1603|1603]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – [[:en:日本橋|Nihonbashi]] in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 19th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1642|1642]] [[:en:April 2|April 2]]) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of [[:en:Kanjō bugyō|Kanjō Bugyō]]. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1854|1854]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the [[:en:Convention of Kanagawa|Nippon-America Treaty]] (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1860|1860]] [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]] was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * [[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the [[:en:Sekihōtai|Akahōtai]], as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * [[:en:Shōō (Kamakura period)|Shōō]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1288|1288]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – [[:en:Emperor Go-Fushimi|Later Fushimi Tennō]], the 93rd [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor of Japan]] (died [[:en:1336|1336]]). * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 18th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1549|1549]] [[:en:March 31|March 31]]) – [[:en:Tsutsui Junkei|Tsuchi no Junkei]], a Sengoku daimyō (died [[:en:1584|1584]]). * [[:en:Kan'en|Kannen]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1749|1749]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – [[:en:Ōta Nanpo|Ōta Nan-Ō]], a playwright (died [[:en:1823|1823]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1825|1825]] [[:en:April 20|April 20]]) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died [[:en:1879|1879]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1832|1832]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died [[:en:1914|1914]]). * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1849|1849]] [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Hirata Tosuke|Hirata Tōsuke]], a bureaucrat and politician (died [[:en:1925|1925]]). * 嘉永4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 4|April 4]]) – [[:en:Ogino Ginko|Ogino Ginko]], a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died [[:en:1913|1913]]). * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1855|1855]] [[:en:April 19|April 19]]) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died [[:en:1924|1924]]). * 安政6th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1859|1859]] [[:en:April 5|April 5]]) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died [[:en:1894|1894]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – [[:en:Itaya Hazan|Itaya Hazan]], a ceramic artist (died [[:en:1963|1963]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Tenbun|Tenbun]] 20th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1551|1551]] [[:en:April 8|April 8]]) – [[:en:Oda Nobuhide|Oda Nobuhide]], a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born [[:en:1510|1510]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 4th year (leap March) ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1599|1599]] [[:en:April 27|April 27]]) – [[:en:Maeda Toshiie|Maeda Toshiie]], a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born [[:en:1538|1538]]). * 安政7th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1860, [[:en:March 24|March 24]]) – [[:en:Ii Naosuke|Ii Naosuke]], Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born [[:en:1815|1815]]). * 慶応4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1868, [[:en:March 26|March 26]]) – [[:en:Sagara Sōzō|Sagara Sōzō]], a Sonnō jōi activist (born [[:en:1839|1839]]).
== Commemorations == * [[:en:Double Third Festival|Shōmiji]] and [[:en:Hinamatsuri|Hina Matsuri]] * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuantian Shangdi]]. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|2|3|4|2|3|4|3|0303|qq=}}

== Commemorations == * {{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|WD=Q698091|lt=Shōmiji}} and {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|WD=Q844110|lt=Hina Matsuri}} * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|en|Xuanwu (god)|WD=Q704070|lt=Xuantian Shangdi}}. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|2|3|4|2|3|4|3|0303|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 7 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月7日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 7일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 7 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初七]][[zh:三月初七]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 7 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月7日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 7일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 7 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初七]][[zh:三月初七]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 7 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月7日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 7일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 7 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初七]][[zh:三月初七]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839096]] Wikidata name: 7th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 7|qq=March 7}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) caused the fall of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Kashida Katsuie}}’s forces by capturing the castle at {{ill|Ise|en|Ise|WD=Q297053|lt=Ise Province}}’s {{ill|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|en|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|WD=Q846676|lt=Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)}} in a prelude to the {{ill|Battle of Shizugatake|en|Battle of Shizugatake|WD=Q225562|lt=Battle of Shizugatake}}. * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603}} {{ill|April 14|en|April 14|WD=Q2500|lt=April 14}}) – {{ill|日本橋|en|日本橋|WD=Q1305451|lt=Nihonbashi}} in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1642|en|1642|WD=Q6912|lt=1642}} {{ill|April 2|en|April 2|WD=Q2511|lt=April 2}}) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of {{ill|Kanjō bugyō|en|Kanjō bugyō|WD=Q1049618|lt=Kanjō Bugyō}}. * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1854|en|1854|WD=Q7670|lt=1854}} {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|WD=Q2461|lt=March 31}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the {{ill|Convention of Kanagawa|en|Convention of Kanagawa|WD=Q771451|lt=Nippon-America Treaty}} (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1860|en|1860|WD=Q7691|lt=1860}} {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24}}) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}} was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the {{ill|Sekihōtai|en|Sekihōtai|WD=Q717217|lt=Akahōtai}}, as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * {{ill|Shōō (Kamakura period)|en|Shōō (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1327371|lt=Shōō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1288|en|1288|WD=Q5550|lt=1288}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Emperor Go-Fushimi|en|Emperor Go-Fushimi|WD=Q440454|lt=Later Fushimi Tennō}}, the 93rd {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}} (died {{ill|1336|en|1336|WD=Q6028|lt=1336}}). * {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 18th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1549|en|1549|WD=Q6554|lt=1549}} {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|WD=Q2461|lt=March 31}}) – {{ill|Tsutsui Junkei|en|Tsutsui Junkei|WD=Q8192220|lt=Tsuchi no Junkei}}, a Sengoku daimyō (died {{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}). * {{ill|Kan'en|en|Kan'en|WD=Q1145723|lt=Kannen}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1749|en|1749|WD=Q7607|lt=1749}} {{ill|April 19|en|April 19|WD=Q2523|lt=April 19}}) – {{ill|Ōta Nanpo|en|Ōta Nanpo|WD=Q386183|lt=Ōta Nan-Ō}}, a playwright (died {{ill|1823|en|1823|WD=Q6988|lt=1823}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1825|en|1825|WD=Q6997|lt=1825}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}}) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}}). * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}}) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died {{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914}}). * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1849|en|1849|WD=Q7650|lt=1849}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – {{ill|Hirata Tosuke|en|Hirata Tosuke|WD=Q3136218|lt=Hirata Tōsuke}}, a bureaucrat and politician (died {{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925}}). * 嘉永4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1851|en|1851|WD=Q7656|lt=1851}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4}}) – {{ill|Ogino Ginko|en|Ogino Ginko|WD=Q4794092|lt=Ogino Ginko}}, a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}}). * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1855|en|1855|WD=Q7673|lt=1855}} {{ill|April 19|en|April 19|WD=Q2523|lt=April 19}}) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}}). * 安政6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1859|en|1859|WD=Q7687|lt=1859}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died {{ill|1894|en|1894|WD=Q7843|lt=1894}}). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 10|en|April 10|WD=Q2502|lt=April 10}}) – {{ill|Itaya Hazan|en|Itaya Hazan|WD=Q11532716|lt=Itaya Hazan}}, a ceramic artist (died {{ill|1963|en|1963|WD=Q2755|lt=1963}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 20th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1551|en|1551|WD=Q6560|lt=1551}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|WD=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide}}, a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born {{ill|1510|en|1510|WD=Q6182|lt=1510}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4th year (leap March) ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1599|en|1599|WD=Q6803|lt=1599}} {{ill|April 27|en|April 27|WD=Q2533|lt=April 27}}) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}}, a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born {{ill|1538|en|1538|WD=Q6470|lt=1538}}). * 安政7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1860, {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24}}) – {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}, Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born {{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815}}). * 慶応4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1868, {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – {{ill|Sagara Sōzō|en|Sagara Sōzō|WD=Q845660|lt=Sagara Sōzō}}, a Sonnō jōi activist (born {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}).

== Commemorations == * {{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|WD=Q698091|lt=Shōmiji}} and {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|WD=Q844110|lt=Hina Matsuri}} * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|en|Xuanwu (god)|WD=Q704070|lt=Xuantian Shangdi}}. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 20th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1551|en|1551|WD=Q6560|lt=1551}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|WD=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide}}, a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born {{ill|1510|en|1510|WD=Q6182|lt=1510}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4th year (leap March) ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1599|en|1599|WD=Q6803|lt=1599}} {{ill|April 27|en|April 27|WD=Q2533|lt=April 27}}) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}}, a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born {{ill|1538|en|1538|WD=Q6470|lt=1538}}). * 安政7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1860, {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24}}) – {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}, Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born {{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815}}). * 慶応4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1868, {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – {{ill|Sagara Sōzō|en|Sagara Sōzō|WD=Q845660|lt=Sagara Sōzō}}, a Sonnō jōi activist (born {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}).
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|WD=Q698091|lt=Shōmiji}} and {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|WD=Q844110|lt=Hina Matsuri}} * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|en|Xuanwu (god)|WD=Q704070|lt=Xuantian Shangdi}}. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.
