{{Not to be confused with|March 7|qq=March 7}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) caused the fall of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Kashida Katsuie}}’s forces by capturing the castle at {{ill|Ise|en|Ise|WD=Q297053|lt=Ise Province}}’s {{ill|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|en|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|WD=Q846676|lt=Kameyama Castle (Ise Province)}} in a prelude to the {{ill|Battle of Shizugatake|en|Battle of Shizugatake|WD=Q225562|lt=Battle of Shizugatake}}. * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603}} {{ill|April 14|en|April 14|WD=Q2500|lt=April 14}}) – {{ill|日本橋|en|日本橋|WD=Q1305451|lt=Nihonbashi}} in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1642|en|1642|WD=Q6912|lt=1642}} {{ill|April 2|en|April 2|WD=Q2511|lt=April 2}}) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of {{ill|Kanjō bugyō|en|Kanjō bugyō|WD=Q1049618|lt=Kanjō Bugyō}}. * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1854|en|1854|WD=Q7670|lt=1854}} {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|WD=Q2461|lt=March 31}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the {{ill|Convention of Kanagawa|en|Convention of Kanagawa|WD=Q771451|lt=Nippon-America Treaty}} (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1860|en|1860|WD=Q7691|lt=1860}} {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24}}) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}} was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the {{ill|Sekihōtai|en|Sekihōtai|WD=Q717217|lt=Akahōtai}}, as pseudo-officials.
== Births == * {{ill|Shōō (Kamakura period)|en|Shōō (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1327371|lt=Shōō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1288|en|1288|WD=Q5550|lt=1288}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Emperor Go-Fushimi|en|Emperor Go-Fushimi|WD=Q440454|lt=Later Fushimi Tennō}}, the 93rd {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan}} (died {{ill|1336|en|1336|WD=Q6028|lt=1336}}). * {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 18th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1549|en|1549|WD=Q6554|lt=1549}} {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|WD=Q2461|lt=March 31}}) – {{ill|Tsutsui Junkei|en|Tsutsui Junkei|WD=Q8192220|lt=Tsuchi no Junkei}}, a Sengoku daimyō (died {{ill|1584|en|1584|WD=Q6728|lt=1584}}). * {{ill|Kan'en|en|Kan'en|WD=Q1145723|lt=Kannen}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1749|en|1749|WD=Q7607|lt=1749}} {{ill|April 19|en|April 19|WD=Q2523|lt=April 19}}) – {{ill|Ōta Nanpo|en|Ōta Nanpo|WD=Q386183|lt=Ōta Nan-Ō}}, a playwright (died {{ill|1823|en|1823|WD=Q6988|lt=1823}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1825|en|1825|WD=Q6997|lt=1825}} {{ill|April 20|en|April 20|WD=Q2524|lt=April 20}}) – {{ill|Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja|不知火光右ヱ門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiranui Kōuenemon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 11th Yokozuna (died {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}}). * {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}}) – {{ill|Matsunomon Sansōko|ja|松の門三艸子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunomon Sansōko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a waka poet and geisha (died {{ill|1914|en|1914|WD=Q2083|lt=1914}}). * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1849|en|1849|WD=Q7650|lt=1849}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – {{ill|Hirata Tosuke|en|Hirata Tosuke|WD=Q3136218|lt=Hirata Tōsuke}}, a bureaucrat and politician (died {{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925}}). * 嘉永4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1851|en|1851|WD=Q7656|lt=1851}} {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4}}) – {{ill|Ogino Ginko|en|Ogino Ginko|WD=Q4794092|lt=Ogino Ginko}}, a feminist and Japan’s first female doctor (died {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}}). * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1855|en|1855|WD=Q7673|lt=1855}} {{ill|April 19|en|April 19|WD=Q2523|lt=April 19}}) – {{ill|Sugiura Shigetake|ja|杉浦重剛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugiura Shigetake|1=Sugiura Shigetake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1188462}}, a thinker and educator (died {{ill|1924|en|1924|WD=Q2173|lt=1924}}). * 安政6th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1859|en|1859|WD=Q7687|lt=1859}} {{ill|April 5|en|April 5|WD=Q2507|lt=April 5}}) – {{ill|Yasui Rimin|ja|安井理民|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasui Rimin|1=Yasui Harutami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450224}}, founder of the first Sōbu Railway (died {{ill|1894|en|1894|WD=Q7843|lt=1894}}). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 10|en|April 10|WD=Q2502|lt=April 10}}) – {{ill|Itaya Hazan|en|Itaya Hazan|WD=Q11532716|lt=Itaya Hazan}}, a ceramic artist (died {{ill|1963|en|1963|WD=Q2755|lt=1963}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun}} 20th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1551|en|1551|WD=Q6560|lt=1551}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8}}) – {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|WD=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide}}, a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born {{ill|1510|en|1510|WD=Q6182|lt=1510}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 4th year (leap March) ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1599|en|1599|WD=Q6803|lt=1599}} {{ill|April 27|en|April 27|WD=Q2533|lt=April 27}}) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie}}, a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born {{ill|1538|en|1538|WD=Q6470|lt=1538}}). * 安政7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1860, {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24}}) – {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke}}, Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born {{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815}}). * 慶応4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1868, {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26}}) – {{ill|Sagara Sōzō|en|Sagara Sōzō|WD=Q845660|lt=Sagara Sōzō}}, a Sonnō jōi activist (born {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}).

== Commemorations == * {{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|WD=Q698091|lt=Shōmiji}} and {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|WD=Q844110|lt=Hina Matsuri}} * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Hama-ori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|en|Xuanwu (god)|WD=Q704070|lt=Xuantian Shangdi}}. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

== Commemorations == * {{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|WD=Q698091|lt=Shōmiji|1=Double Third Festival}} and {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|WD=Q844110|lt=Hina Matsuri|1=Hinamatsuri}} * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Q11557526|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|en|Xuanwu (god)|WD=Q704070|lt=Xuantian Shangdi|1=Xuanwu (god)}}. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583|1=1583}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24|1=April 24}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) caused the fall of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Kashida Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}}’s forces by capturing the castle at {{ill|Ise|en|Ise|WD=Q297053|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise}}’s {{ill|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|en|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|WD=Q846676|lt=Kameyama Castle {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} Kanagawa|en|Convention of Kanagawa|WD=Q771451|lt=Nippon-America Treaty|1=Convention of Kanagawa}} (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke|1=Ii Naosuke}} was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初七|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 7일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 7''' is the 7th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1583|en|1583|WD=Q6726|lt=1583|1=1583}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24|1=April 24}}) – {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|羽柴秀吉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q187550}} (later known as {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}) caused the fall of {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|WD=Q707587|lt=Kashida Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}}’s forces by capturing the castle at {{ill|Ise|en|Ise|WD=Q297053|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise}}’s {{ill|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|en|Kameyama Castle (Mie)|WD=Q846676|lt=Kameyama Castle {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1603|en|1603|WD=Q6735|lt=1603|1=1603}} {{ill|April 14|en|April 14|WD=Q2500|lt=April 14|1=April 14}}) – {{ill|日本橋|en|日本橋|WD=Q1305451|lt=Nihonbashi|1=日本橋}} in Chūō, Tokyo, was opened. * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 19th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1642|en|1642|WD=Q6912|lt=1642|1=1642}} {{ill|April 2|en|April 2|WD=Q2511|lt=April 2|1=April 2}}) – The {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} established the office of {{ill|Kanjō bugyō|en|Kanjō bugyō|WD=Q1049618|lt=Kanjō Bugyō|1=Kanjō bugyō}}. * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1854|en|1854|WD=Q7670|lt=1854|1=1854}} {{ill|March 31|en|March 31|WD=Q2461|lt=March 31|1=March 31}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate and U.S. envoy Perry signed the {{ill|Convention of Kanagawa|en|Convention of Kanagawa|WD=Q771451|lt=Nippon-America Treaty|1=Convention of Kanagawa}} (Kanagawa Treaty) in Yokohama, ending Japan’s national seclusion. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1860|en|1860|WD=Q7691|lt=1860|1=1860}} {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24|1=March 24}}) – During the [[:en:Sakuradamon Incident|桜田門外の変]], Tairō {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke|1=Ii Naosuke}} was attacked and killed by rōnin outside Sakurada Gate of Edo Castle. * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26|1=March 26}}) – The Higashiyama Road Vanguard Government executed officials, including members of the {{ill|Sekihōtai|en|Sekihōtai|WD=Q717217|lt=Akahōtai|1=Sekihōtai}}, as pseudo-officials.

== Deaths == * {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|WD=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 20th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1551|en|1551|WD=Q6560|lt=1551|1=1551}} {{ill|April 8|en|April 8|WD=Q2503|lt=April 8|1=April 8}}) – {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|WD=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide|1=Oda Nobuhide}}, a military commander and father of Oda Nobunaga (born {{ill|1510|en|1510|WD=Q6182|lt=1510|1=1510}}). * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 4th year (leap March) ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1599|en|1599|WD=Q6803|lt=1599|1=1599}} {{ill|April 27|en|April 27|WD=Q2533|lt=April 27|1=April 27}}) – {{ill|Maeda Toshiie|en|Maeda Toshiie|WD=Q1196817|lt=Maeda Toshiie|1=Maeda Toshiie}}, a military commander and one of the Tairō; he was the first lord of the Kaga Domain (born {{ill|1538|en|1538|WD=Q6470|lt=1538|1=1538}}). * 安政7th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} 1860, {{ill|March 24|en|March 24|WD=Q2453|lt=March 24|1=March 24}}) – {{ill|Ii Naosuke|en|Ii Naosuke|WD=Q716940|lt=Ii Naosuke|1=Ii Naosuke}}, Tairō of the Tokugawa shogunate (born {{ill|1815|en|1815|WD=Q6948|lt=1815|1=1815}}). * 慶応4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} 1868, {{ill|March 26|en|March 26|WD=Q2456|lt=March 26|1=March 26}}) – {{ill|Sagara Sōzō|en|Sagara Sōzō|WD=Q845660|lt=Sagara Sōzō|1=Sagara Sōzō}}, a Sonnō jōi activist (born {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839|1=1839}}).
== Commemorations == * {{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|WD=Q698091|lt=Shōmiji|1=Double Third Festival}} and {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|WD=Q844110|lt=Hina Matsuri|1=Hinamatsuri}} * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Q11557526|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|en|Xuanwu (god)|WD=Q704070|lt=Xuantian Shangdi|1=Xuanwu (god)}}. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.

== Commemorations == * {{ill|Double Third Festival|en|Double Third Festival|qid=Q698091|lt=Shōmiji|1=Double Third Festival}} and {{ill|Hinamatsuri|en|Hinamatsuri|qid=Q844110|lt=Hina Matsuri|1=Hinamatsuri}} * {{ill|Hama-ori|ja|浜下り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hama-ori|1=Q11557526|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557526}} – In Okinawa, women purify themselves by presenting food in tiered boxes at the beach; this tradition is combined with clam digging. * 玄天上帝誕辰 – The birthday celebration of the Taoist deity {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|en|Xuanwu (god)|qid=Q704070|lt=Xuantian Shangdi|1=Xuanwu (god)}}. * A traditional event of the Zhuang people in China (in Zhuang language: samnyiedcosam) to honor their ancestors, accompanied by large-scale singing gatherings. * An independent New Year celebration by the Khor Lao ethnic group in Vietnam.
